73755.subject: $1.1B Stolen in DeFi. Institutions Still Want In
73755.body:
**gm, Defiers!**

These were our top news today:

* **[Crypto](https://thedefiant.io/news/markets/crypto-liquidations-near-1-billion-24-hours-g13uhz)**[ liquidations near $1B in 24 hours as leverage unwinds](https://thedefiant.io/news/markets/crypto-liquidations-near-1-billion-24-hours-g13uhz)

* **[Base](https://thedefiant.io/news/blockchains/base-launches-azul-upgrade-takes-step-toward-stage-2-decentralization)**[ hits Stage 2 with multiproofs, 99% fewer empty blocks](https://thedefiant.io/news/blockchains/base-launches-azul-upgrade-takes-step-toward-stage-2-decentralization)

* **[Vitalik](https://thedefiant.io/news/people/vitalik-buterin-endorses-interfold-privacy-protocol-voting-auctions-m4ldfh)**[ endorses Interfold, ZK+FHE privacy stack for voting and auctions](https://thedefiant.io/news/people/vitalik-buterin-endorses-interfold-privacy-protocol-voting-auctions-m4ldfh)

* **[Tempo L1](https://thedefiant.io/news/blockchains/tempo-l1-hits-3-9m-transactions-in-two-months)**[ hits 3.9M transactions in two months, $25M+ stablecoin supply](https://thedefiant.io/news/blockchains/tempo-l1-hits-3-9m-transactions-in-two-months)

It was a bit of a slow news day, but the standout for me was the [livestream discussion](https://x.com/i/broadcasts/1jxXggbEnMyJZ) we held on @DefiantNews X this morning: Are Institutions still coming to DeFi after the latest string of hacks?

As we [reported yesterday](https://thedefiant.io/news/hacks/openzeppelin-pushes-back-after-ex-cto-declares-all-of-defi-unsafe), Manuel Araoz, the co-founder of OpenZeppelin, the security shop which reviewed Aave, Compound, and Maker, posted that he now considers **"all of DeFi unsafe,"** arguing that AI coding agents have turned vulnerability-hunting into a game defenders structurally can't win. The backdrop is ugly: **roughly $630M drained across 27 exploits in April alone, $1.1B over twelve months, KelpDAO down $292M to Lazarus, Drift down $285M.**

So I sat down with John Zettler of Kraken, Sun Raghupathi of Veda, and Anthony DeMartino of Sentora, three people whose entire business is convincing institutions to route capital into this stuff, and asked: Are your phones ringing with panic, or worse, is it just crickets?

**The opposite, it turns out.**

"My phone has been blowing up since our launch," Zettler said of Kraken's new Bitcoin vault, which crossed $60M in TVL within a day. Sentora's DeMartino said his firm "landed three deals in the last five weeks," during the worst stretch of hacks, when he'd expected a pullback. **"The conversations have heated up, not slowed down since the incidents in April. The coincidence is hard to explain."**

Here's my attempt to explain it: **The hacks aren't deterring institutions because they changed what kind of risk DeFi is.**

As Sun put it, **"risk is now moving from the smart contract layer into the operational layer."** Almost none of the recent disasters were broken math in a contract. They were compromised keys,

Caption:

Caption: 

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/6e77f043-cacc-443d-8fd9-80f5444b8e13/unnamed.jpg?t=1780000425)
Caption: 

# Thursday links: Event contracts are commodities, too

[The SDNY policies prediction markets](https://www.justice.gov/usao-sdny/pr/google-employee-charged-insider-trading)

Michele Spagnuolo has been charged with commodities fraud in the Southern District of New York. 

Spagnuolo, we learn, is an Italian national who lives and works in Switzerland (which is not in New York) and was accused of fraudulently trading event contracts related to celebrity Google searches (which are commodities, apparently) on Polymarket’s global prediction market (which is offshore and unregulated).

All this will be adjudicated in a New York courtroom because, [the complaint](https://www.justice.gov/usao-sdny/media/1442621/dl) says, “Polymarket’s website is accessible via the internet throughout the world, including in the Southern District of New York.”

Spagnuolo accessed that website from Switzerland, and there’s no indication his counterparties were in New York either. (If so, they’d have had to sneak on to the site with a VPN: Polymarket’s global exchange is geofenced — albeit weakly — to keep Americans out.)

But US prosecutors nonetheless claim jurisdiction.

==“Today’s charges reinforce a decades-old message: Corporate insiders cannot use confidential business information to turn a profit in our markets,” US Attorney Jay Clayton said.  ==

_Our_== markets!==

A prediction-market crime anywhere, it seems, is a prediction-market crime in New York. 

[He did a TED Talk](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QTGyQKaTx8c)

On his [personal website](https://miki.it/#skills), Spagnuolo notes that he’s the co-author of a JavaScript specification that ==“protects more than a third of the internet's HTML traffic against Cross-Site Scripting (XSS).” ==

==Impressive!==

At Google he’s an engineer in charge of “the Agent & Web Observability area in the Information Security team.” (Or was, at least. His [Google Research page](https://research.google/people/michelespagnuolo/) has been scrubbed.)== ==

==On ==[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/michelespagnuolo/)== he shares that he achieved a 4.0 GPA in computer science at the University of Illinois. At the Polytechnic University of Milan, he scored a perfect 110 out of 110 in computer engineering.==

How such incredibly smart people can do

73751.subject: OpenAI Is Coming for Google’s Turf With AI Ads
73751.body:
Daily Pre-IPO Briefing

StartEngine Private (https://dxszHp04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/DR+113/dxszHp04/VXfyp84d3WPJW8p5b7w6GNG5dVtG5yr5PD0Q8N5_C1mM3lcq-W6N1vHY6lZ3lSW78Cb2D1bHlhdW6mBqw032kZFcW2stJbY51_6stW44R02Q3djNlzW2zBdXp2dVZmTW8lxWXx65NK93N2x3HCcFDkz-VpBJl_2YK00mW3FHcFs20_ykbW2g37QS3z2TQ8N32xlXkND1k4W6fnJ6T7FHHBPW3M2hRp2Dx5FtW2QMwgC3KrFJcW45_Zh61gDZS5W48sRYZ6JkZ5CN2bMfFQvNBc_W4CFZwb3cbPRHW3q_V_02LHD_tW71LfNK7dqFXNW20NL144WpZ25W33fgSP5B69r0f5j08Db04 )

OpenAI (https://dxszHp04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/DR+113/dxszHp04/VXfyp84d3WPJW8p5b7w6GNG5dVtG5yr5PD0Q8N5_C1mM3lcq-W6N1vHY6lZ3lSW78Cb2D1bHlhdW6mBqw032kZFcW2stJbY51_6stW44R02Q3djNlzW2zBdXp2dVZmTW8lxWXx65NK93N2x3HCcFDkz-VpBJl_2YK00mW3FHcFs20_ykbW2g37QS3z2TQ8N32xlXkND1k4W6fnJ6T7FHHBPW3M2hRp2Dx5FtW2QMwgC3KrFJcW45_Zh61gDZS5W48sRYZ6JkZ5CN2bMfFQvNBc_W4CFZwb3cbPRHW3q_V_02LHD_tW71LfNK7dqFXNW20NL144WpZ25W33fgSP5B69r0f5j08Db04 )

Hi Oleg,

Welcome back to our Daily Pre-IPO Briefing, your quick recap of major headlines involving companies our investors already have exposure to via StartEngine Private.

Today’s theme? Three private-market sectors may be entering their next phase:

- Kalshi & Polymarket received a positive signal for potential regulatory relief. The White House is reviewing a new proposal from the Commodity Futures Trading Commission, which would lay out federal guidelines for event contracts. The current administration has been working to wrest oversight of prediction markets from state gaming commissions.¹
- OpenAI’s move into ads could potentially challenge Google's search empire. Hundreds of companies have bought ads on ChatGPT, including HubSpot, Cursor, and Indeed. These ads are built around conversations with users instead of keywords, which researchers believe could have enormous implications for how AI ads are targeted, priced, and monetized.²
- PsiQuantum opened a research hub at Griffith University in Brisbane, Australia on Wednesday. It’s the first such facility outside of the northern hemisphere. PsiQuantum plans to test and prove technologies vital for its long-planned quantum computer facility.³

A confidential filing does not guarantee that an IPO will occur. These reports don't imply specific results for investors with exposure to these companies on StartEngine Private. Past performance is not indicative of future results. There's no guarantee that revenue, funding, or valuation will continue to grow, or that these companies will be profitable. Kalshi and Polymarket have been the subject of state and federal scrutiny. Future legal or regulatory developments could impact the companies’ operations. See important risk disclosures on the respective offering pages.

From prediction markets to AI ads to quantum computing, some of the biggest private-market companies appear focused on reshaping entire industries, not just competing within them.

Connect with our team to discuss the latest trends, or explore our current offerings.

Explore Pre-IPO Opportunities

73274.subject: Svadobná výzdoba
73274.body:
Zdravíme nevestu💍🤍,

Ďakujeme veľmi pekne za vašu správu.. Termín svadby máme ešte voľný a veľmi
radi budeme súčasťou vašej svadby.🤍

Spravidla sa s klientami stretávame osobne v showroome v Rozhanovciach
(kúsok od Košíc) kde si spolu vieme vyskladať svadobnú výzdobu a na základe
toho vytvoriť cenovú ponuku na mieru pre vás ak by ste mali záujem prosím
navrhnite deň a čas kedy by vám to najviac vyhovovalo aby sa časovo radi
prispôsobíme vášmu času. Poprípade, ak sme mimo Košíc či Prešova, radi si s
Vami dohodneme videohovor. Prosím navrhnite deň a čas, ktorý by Vám
vyhovoval najviac, pokojne aj cez víkend aj vo večerných hodinách. 🙂
Najbližsie voľne termíny máme ešte aj tento týždeň. Forme stretnutia sa
radi prispôsobíme Vám, čo je pre Vás praktickejšie ☺️

Budeme sa tešiť na prípadnú spoluprácu s Vami.

S pozdravom

Lenka Gattiker,
Vaša osobná posh asistentka
